preconditions: TASK-000002 fresh-host evidence exists or the lab is explicitly same-host and no-claim
TASK-000003 authority boundaries are reviewed or the updater lab is isolated
TASK-000007 migration terms are available before any profile schema rollback fixture exists
checked no-claim update-lab package template remains current

checked no-claim research package/update readiness-review template remains current and cannot be cited for release readiness, supported security, production updater, stable-channel, public-distribution, signing-readiness, or implementation evidence
no production signing keys, offline root keys, real updater, stable channel, or public binary distribution are in scope
queue status remains proposed; this specified manifest is non-executable
a named owner and named independent reviewer replace the role placeholders before this manifest can become ready
acceptance_criteria: checked no-claim research package/update lab inventory is treated as planning evidence until executable manifests and update tests exist

first executable update-lab package starts from the checked no-claim update-lab package template and replaces template-only fields with real package-manifest, metadata-parser, fake-key signature-threshold, staged-install, rollback/migration, privacy-event, cleanup, and owner-review evidence

first research package/update readiness review starts from the checked no-claim research package/update readiness-review template and replaces template-only null lab_package, reference_platform, fixture_policy, owner_reviewer, independent_reviewer, release_operations_reviewer, security_reviewer, and privacy_reviewer fields with owner-reviewed evidence beyond the template

signed research-package manifest binds source commit, build ID, channel, platform, architecture, toolchain, feature set, SBOM, provenance, symbols, notices, artifact hashes, artifact sizes, and no-stable-support label

update metadata lab covers role separation, signature threshold, expiry, minimum secure version, channel, platform, architecture, rollout, mirrors, staged install, tamper, replay, wrong-target, partial-write, disk-full, and power-loss tests

rollback and migration lab proves authorized known-good rollback only, revoked or vulnerable version refusal, profile schema compatibility, downgrade behavior, crash-loop health gate, and privacy-preserving local event record
release, security, storage, and readiness documents keep unsupported production signing, offline root, stable-channel, user-profile migration, real-updater, and public-distribution behavior explicit
negative_tests: tamper, replay, wrong-target, expiry, mirror, partial-write, disk-full, and power-loss cases cannot be reported as successful updates
rollback cannot restore revoked or vulnerable versions
the lab cannot use production signing keys, offline root keys, stable-channel infrastructure, public distribution, or real user profile data
research-package evidence cannot create a production update, stable support, security, or release claim

checked no-claim update-lab package template cannot be cited as executable package manifest, update metadata parser, signature-threshold, staged-install, rollback safety, migration safety, release readiness, supported security, or production updater evidence

checked no-claim research package/update readiness-review template cannot be cited as release readiness, supported security, production updater, stable-channel, public-distribution, signing-readiness, owner-review, or implementation evidence

rollback.strategy: Revert lab manifests, tools, and readiness wording if update verification or rollback semantics are ambiguous.
